news.summary: KBRA has issued its European Auto ABS Indices for April 2025, showcasing the latest credit trends in securitised auto loans and leases. The report offers insights for investors looking to navigate this market. KBRA, a prominent credit rating agency, operates in the U.S., EU, and the UK, and holds the status of Qualified Rating Agency in Taiwan. Additionally, it is a designated Rating Organization for structured finance ratings in Canada. With a broad reach, KBRA serves as a valuable resource for investors seeking reliable credit ratings.
news.headline: KBRA releases European Auto ABS Indices for April 2025, offering credit trends for securitised auto loans. Check report for details.

news.summary: Bitcoin buyer interest is at all-time highs, with the price uptrend expected to continue according to CryptoQuant analysis. Bitcoin short-term holders are seeing profits as the market surges, with buy orders dominating and sellers ceding control. The 90-day cumulative volume delta (CVD) favors Bitcoin bulls, indicating potential for further upward movement. Hodlers are holding off on selling, with daily profit-taking lower compared to when Bitcoin first hit $100,000 in 2024. Older coins are less active, showing stronger holding behavior. Price momentum increased after reclaiming the average cost basis for Bitcoin's short-term holders at just under $100,000. Bitcoin is rallying, and readers are advised to conduct their own research before making investment decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin buyer dominance at $111K signals potential for further gains as CryptoQuant analysis suggests uptrend may continue. BTC holders refrain from selling at current levels, with older coins showing stronger holding behavior. Price momentum increases after reclaiming average cost basis for short-term holders at just under $100K.

news.summary: Anton Bukov, co-founder of decentralized exchange 1inch, highlighted that traditional banks spend $100-$300 per user for onboarding, while online banks spend $20-$30. DeFi, on the other hand, requires minimal costs, giving it an advantage in reaching the 1.4 billion unbanked people globally. Bukov emphasized that DeFi allows the unbanked to access the global economy easily using stablecoins like Tether. He expressed that DeFi serves as a tool for financial inclusion and provides access to global liquidity. Bukov mentioned that countries aligning regulations with this new economic trend can benefit from capital movement and cooperation.
news.headline: DeFi enables 1.4B unbanked access with minimal costs; traditional banks spend $100-$300 per user, DeFi requires near-zero onboarding expense.

This event signifies Moldova's prominent position in the wine industry and offers unique insights into the country's viticulture practices.
news.headline: Moldova to host 46th World Congress of Vine and Wine, known for highest vineyard density per capita (4 hectares).

news.summary: Tom Erik Foss-Jacobsen has been named the new CEO of Borregaard ASA, taking over from Per A. Sørlie who is retiring after holding the position for 26 years, as announced on December 3, 2020, to the Oslo Stock Exchange. Foss-Jacobsen will assume the role on August 1, 2025.
news.headline: Tom Erik Foss-Jacobsen named CEO of Borregaard ASA, succeeding Per A. Sørlie after 26 years. Transition effective 1 August 2025.

news.summary: In the recent financial report, MINISO mainland China experienced a notable revenue increase of 18.9% compared to the previous year. The March Quarter demonstrated a substantial sequential growth in same-store sales. The gross margin also showed a positive trend, reaching 44.2%, an increase of 0.8 percentage points year over year. Additionally, the adjusted EBITDA surged by 7.5% from the previous year, totaling RMB1,037.3 million. Overall, shareholder returns saw a promising uptrend.
news.headline: MINISO Group's revenue grew by 18.9% to RMB1,037.3 million in March Quarter 2025 with gross margin reaching 44.2%. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 7.5% year over year.

news.summary: Investors are currently analyzing the potential effects of the "One Big Beautiful Bill Act," but an expert is cautioning them to also consider the U.S. tariff policy. Bob Elliott, the CIO of Unlimited Funds, believes that the stock market is not fully pricing in the risks associated with tariff rates and argues that understanding the intricacies of this policy is crucial. Despite recent progress with a "China deal," Elliott notes that tariff rates remain high, raising questions about whether further relief is expected or if the U.S. will revert to higher tariffs. The economic consequences of these decisions could vary significantly. This insight was shared in a post on May 22, 2025, and the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Expert Bob Elliott warns stock market is underpricing tariff risks, says nuances more crucial than legislative package. Despite recent China deal, tariff rates still high, uncertain. Economic impacts could be significant.

news.summary: The latest data shows that as of today, there have been a total of 250,000 confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the country. Out of these cases, 50,000 have resulted in hospitalization, with 10,000 requiring intensive care. The death toll stands at 5,000. The government has implemented various measures to combat the spread of the virus, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and restrictions on large gatherings.Officials are urging the public to continue practicing social distancing and proper hygiene to help flatten the curve.
news.headline: Decisions at the AGM of AS Ekspress Grupp on the 23rd May 2025 were approved by shareholders.

news.summary: The European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation sets stringent rules for stablecoin issuers in the EU. To trade in the EU, stablecoin issuers must acquire a license to become an electronic money institution, hold 60% of reserves in EU-based banks, provide full transparency through disclosures, and comply with other regulations. Tether, a significant stablecoin issuer with its USDT, rejected MiCA regulations citing concerns about banking regulations, distrust in the digital euro, and prioritizing users in countries like Brazil, Turkey, and Nigeria. Exchanges like Binance and Kraken have delisted USDT trading pairs for users in the European Economic Area due to non-compliance, affecting liquidity and forcing users towards MiCA-compliant stablecoins like USDC and EURC. The move by Tether to avoid MiCA highlights the regulatory challenges in the global crypto landscape, with companies resorting to regulatory arbitrage by moving to friendlier jurisdictions. Tether is now focusing on El Salvador and diversified investments after rejecting MiCA, illustrating the complex dynamics of global crypto regulations and business operations.
news.headline: Tether resists MiCA for banking, user privacy, and global impact reasons. EU rules reshape crypto space with 60% bank reserves mandate. Exchanges delist USDT in EEA due to non-compliance. Tether finds new opportunities in El Salvador and tech investments amid regulatory challenges. Regulatory arbitrage in crypto industry underlines disjointed global standards. Tether's decision showcases the struggle of complying with varying regulations worldwide.

news.summary: Swedish crypto asset manager Virtune has listed its flagship Virtune XRP ETP on Germany's Deutsche Börse Xetra, expanding its digital asset offerings into Europe's largest economy. Since its establishment in May 2023, Virtune has grown rapidly in the Nordics, listing 16 products and attracting over 140,000 investors. The Virtune XRP ETP, initially listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, has garnered 50,000 investors and over $125 million in assets under management. Coinbase serves as the custodian for the product, ensuring institutional-grade security. Virtune aims to provide regulated access to crypto assets through ETPs, enhancing transparency and investor protection. CEO Christopher Kock highlights the strategic importance of the Xetra listing for Virtune's European expansion and emphasizes the strong demand for XRP as a leading global digital asset.
news.headline: Swedish Virtune launches XRP ETP on Deutsche Börse Xetra, expanding into Germany; over 140,000 investors, $125M AUM.

news.summary: In Sandviken, Sweden, on May 23, 2025, Alleima's Kanthal division, in collaboration with Danieli, has been chosen to provide a pilot-scale electric process gas heater for Emsteel's Direct Reduced Iron (DRI) facility in Abu Dhabi, UAE. The goal is to introduce electric heating to a portion of the current system. This initiative signifies a move towards sustainability and innovation in the industry. This project is a significant step towards reducing carbon emissions and modernizing traditional heating methods. The heater is set to be a crucial component in the plant's operations, marking a shift towards greener practices.
news.headline: Alleima's Kanthal division and Danieli to supply first electric process gas heater for commercial use in DRI; to be installed at Emsteel's DRI plant in UAE.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures surged over 2% in premarket trading on Monday following a 90-day truce between the U.S. and China on reciprocal tariffs. The tariffs were reduced from 125% to 10%, maintaining a 30% overall tariff on China with fentanyl-related tariffs remaining at 20%. Talks held in Geneva over the weekend showed positive progress, leading to a joint statement where Beijing agreed to suspend non-tariff countermeasures. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent praised the talks as productive. Additionally, the 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.44% and the two-year bond was at 3.98%. Markets indicated a 92.1% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in its June meeting. Various index ETFs were higher in premarket, with SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust up 2.74% and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF up 3.81%. Key sectors and companies showed mixed performance on Friday, with EchoStar Corp. rising 1.43% and Plains All American Pipeline LP falling nearly 3%. The Dow Jones index had declined 119 points to 41,249.38.
news.headline: US stock futures rise over 2% on Monday as US-China agree to 90-day tariff pause, reducing reciprocal tariffs from 125% to 10%. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent cites productive trade negotiations in Geneva. 92.1% likelihood of interest rates unchanged in June.

news.summary: The April Consumer Price Index (CPI) report is expected to impact Wall Street's bullish trend following a U.S.-China tariff rollback. The headline inflation rate is forecasted to remain at 2.4%, the 49th consecutive month above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. Inflation is expected to rebound from a 0.1% drop in March to 0.3% in April, with Core CPI projected to rise 0.3% monthly, maintaining a 2.8% annual rate. Bank of America's economist predicts further inflation increases due to front-loaded demand and the April tariff shock impact. The recent U.S.-China trade deal has diminished Fed rate cut expectations, reducing the probability of a 25-basis-point cut in June to 8%. The odds for a July cut are at 42%, with expectations for two cuts by December dropping to 72% from nearly three priced before the last FOMC meeting.
news.headline: Consumer Price Index for April could impact Fed rate cut hopes. Inflation rate remains at 2.4%, with prices expected to rise 0.3% in April. Current odds for a Fed rate cut in June at just 8%, down from nearly 40% last week. Fed may be limited due to inflation hitting target.

news.summary: US stock futures turned positive during Wednesday's premarket session, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indices also in the green for the year after Tuesday's mixed finish. This positivity was driven by a positive inflation report for April and easing trade concerns. Chipmaker Nvidia Corp.'s stock soared due to expected chip regulation changes and a new collaboration with Saudi Arabia. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.45%, while the two-year bond was at 3.99%. The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 91.8% likelihood of the Fed maintaining current interest rates in June. Major index performances included a 0.31% YTD increase for the S&P 500, -0.59% YTD for Dow Jones, and 1.06% YTD for Nasdaq 100. S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF were trading higher premarket. Tuesday saw gains in tech, consumer discretionary, energy, and communication sectors, while healthcare, consumer staples, and real estate declined. The Labor Department reported a 2.3% year-over-year increase in the Consumer Price Index for April. Analysts noted a potential for further gains, with over 60% of S&P 500 components reaching new 20-day highs on Tuesday.
news.headline: US stock futures trade higher on Wednesday following positive inflation report. S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 turned positive for the year. Nvidia Corp. stock surged due to chip regulations overhaul and collaboration with Saudi Arabia. CME Group's FedWatch tool shows 91.8% likelihood of unchanged interest rates. Dow Jones up 0.05%, S&P 500 up 0.22%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.36%. SPY was up 0.22% to $588.11, QQQ advanced 0.37% to $517.48. S&P 500 index up 0.31% YTD, Dow Jones down -0.59% YTD, Nasdaq 100 up 1.06% YTD. Dow Jones index declined 270 points (0.64%) to 42,140.43. S&P 500 index rose 0.72% to 5,886.55. Nasdaq Composite ended 1.61% higher at 19,010.08, Russell 2000 climbed 0.49% to 2,102.35.

news.summary: The trade tension between the U.S. and China eased as both countries agreed to reduce tariffs by 115 percentage points, causing U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods to drop to 30%. This development led to a surge in equity markets, with the S&P 500 reaching levels from early March 2025 and recovering from the impact of President Donald Trump's tariff announcement on "Liberation Day". The more positive trade situation led economists to lower the probability of a recession and upgrade their growth projections for the U.S. in the latter part of the year. The S&P 500 experienced five consecutive winning sessions, with the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) closing the week 5% higher. Semiconductor stocks, represented by the iShares Semiconductor ETF (NYSE: SOXX), saw a notable rise of almost 10% for the week, marking the ETF's fourth consecutive week of gains and the best monthly rally since 2020. On the other hand, clean energy stocks, such as those tracked by the Invesco Solar ETF (NYSE: TAN), outperformed semiconductor stocks. Solar companies experienced a surge in stock prices following the release of an unexpected favorable tax proposal by Republicans. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US-China trade truce cuts tariffs by 115 percentage points, S&P 500 rises to early March 2025 levels, economists upgrade US growth. S&P 500 ETF up 5%, semiconductor and clean energy stocks have strong gains. Democratic Party's tax proposal leads to surge in solar stocks.

news.summary: Latest data released on August 14, 2024, reveals that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) remains stable, indicating a more relaxed inflation rate. This development is priming the Federal Reserve to initiate the process of reducing interest rates starting in September. Economists predict this could lead to significant changes in the economic landscape in the coming months.
news.headline: Inflation calmer, setting up Fed Reserve for rate cuts in Sept. By Lawrence Yun. August 14, 2024.

news.summary: The 2024 NAR Member Profile revealed a noteworthy trend in the real estate industry - new REALTORS® exhibit greater diversity compared to experienced professionals. This indicates positive change in the industry, with a growing number of diverse individuals entering the field. This emphasizes a shift towards inclusivity and representation, with fresh perspectives enriching the real estate sector. The statistics reflect a significant increase in diversity among new NAR members in 2024, marking a progressive shift in the composition of professionals within the industry.
news.headline: 2024 NAR Member Profile shows new REALTORS® are more diverse than experienced ones, bringing fresh perspectives.

news.summary: Mortgage rates for August 15, 2024 showed little change as the 30-year fixed rate held steady at 6.49%, only slightly up from the previous week's 6.47%. According to Freddie Mac data, this marks a marginal increase in borrowing costs for homebuyers. This stability in rates offers some relief to potential homebuyers amid the current market conditions. Expert Jessica Lautz from Mortgages & Home Financing notes the steady nature of these rates, indicating a relatively calm period in the mortgage market. Homebuyers and industry experts are keeping a close eye on these figures as they plan their next moves in the real estate market.
news.headline: 30-year fixed mortgage rate from Freddie Mac remains flat at 6.49% on August 15, 2024, compared to 6.47% last week.

news.summary: Realities of purchasing a home as a single woman are discussed in a recent report. The report explores diverse strategies employed by single women when buying a house, shedding light on the obstacles they encounter. The study delves into various demographic groups, buyer interactions, and the impact of student loan debt on this segment of the housing market. This analysis aims to highlight the unique challenges faced by single women in homeownership.
news.headline: The news article discusses financial challenges for single female home buyers, focusing on student loans.

news.summary: First-time homebuyers are advised to approach the real estate market with caution and awareness. It is essential for them to be demure and mindful in various aspects, including their financial situation, previous living circumstances, the offers they make as buyers, and their interactions with real estate agents. Being prudent and attentive in these areas is crucial for first-time buyers to navigate the real estate landscape effectively. This guidance from real estate expert Jessica Lautz underscores the importance of careful consideration and wise decision-making for individuals venturing into homeownership for the first time.
news.headline: First-time homebuyers need to be demure and mindful in finances, living situation, offers when working with real estate.

news.summary: The latest data shows that housing affordability has improved as mortgage rates have dropped below 6.5%. This decrease in rates has made purchasing a home more accessible to a wider range of buyers. The exact numbers indicate a significant impact on affordability, with more individuals now able to afford a home than before the rate decrease. This shift in the housing market is expected to stimulate more activity among potential buyers as they capitalize on this improved affordability.
news.headline: Housing affordability improving as mortgage rates fall below 6.5%. Insights on who can afford to buy a home.

news.summary: Recent data shows a decrease in average 30-year fixed mortgage rates from Freddie Mac, falling to 6.35% compared to last week's 6.46%. Jessica Lautz discusses this development in the Mortgages & Home Financing sector.
news.headline: 30-year fixed mortgage rate drops to 6.35% from 6.46% last week - Freddie Mac, August 29, 2024.

news.summary: The likelihood of the U.S. going into a recession in 2025 has significantly decreased on prediction platforms due to eased tensions in the U.S.-China trade dispute. Bets on the contract "US recession in 2025?" have fallen from a high of 66% in April to 39% on Polymarket, a platform based on Polygon (CRYPTO: POL) where users can gamble with cryptocurrencies. Currently, approximately $5.5 million has been wagered on this prediction. For the outcome to be determined as "Yes," the economy must experience another quarter of negative growth. For the complete article, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Recession odds under 40% on crypto platform due to US-China trade calm. Bet totals $5.5M. Full story on Benzinga.

news.summary: There has been a notable decrease in home-purchase loan applications nationwide, dropping from 7.9 million to 6.5 million. This trend reflects a change in housing demand as indicated by the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA) data. The data analysis by Anat Nusinovich Mortgages & Home Financing highlights a shift in the housing market dynamics that may impact the overall real estate sector.
news.headline: Home-purchase loan applications drop from 7.9M to 6.5M nationwide; impact of HMDA on housing demand - Anat Nusinovich.

news.summary: The global Music Streaming Market size is projected to reach USD 143.89 billion by 2032, with a 17.3% CAGR from 2025. Key points include: 3X growth from USD 47.06 billion in 2025, on-demand streaming dominating the market, audio content generating half the revenue in 2025, individual users contributing around USD 26.73 billion in 2025. North America leads with USD 20.98 billion in 2025, while Asia Pacific is expected to have the fastest growth. Factors driving growth include increased digital music consumption through smartphones and high-speed internet. Music streaming services like Spotify and Apple Music are gaining popularity over traditional media sources, with convenience and flexibility being key drivers. Piracy and internet access issues may limit market growth.
news.headline: Music streaming market valued at USD 47.06B in 2025, set to reach USD 143.89B by 2032 at 17.3% CAGR, driven by on-demand audio and subscriptions.
